commands.repair_reason: Human handling is needed because real-behavior proof is still insufficient and the proxy/security/API decisions cannot be completed by a repair worker alone.; Needs attention: The diff introduces security-sensitive local TLS proxy behavior and cached signing keys, and GitHub Advanced Security also flagged raw socket egress classification for maintainer review.; Review finding: [P1] Remove the unused Claude helper im... (sha=7d60fb37632a8b1b1fbff16d9948dbf93c5ac0ce)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Claude interactive CLI backend/proxy, emits Claude stream-json thinking and tool events, and renders Telegram reasoning plus tool progress in one rolling message.

PR surface: Source +2423, Tests +233, Config +31. Total +2687 across 31 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: the unused import is visible in the merge-ref source against noUnusedLocals TypeScript settings, and the async tool-event callback is invoked without await or drain in the new bridge. I did not run write-producing tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human handling is needed because real-behavior proof is still insufficient and the proxy/security/API decisions cannot be completed by a repair worker alone.

target.title: feat(telegram): interleave CLI tool-progress + reasoning + rolling timer in one Telegram message
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** When Claude calls a tool mid-turn on the `claude-cli` backend, the assistant text stream just pauses ÔÇö often for tens of seconds on `Read` / `Bash` / `Grep` / `WebFetch`. The end user sees a frozen typing indicator and no signal about which tool is running.
- **Why it matters.** Looks like the gateway hung; users abandon turns that are still in flight. Tool calls live in `stream_event` records, never in `assistant_text` deltas, so the existing assistant-stream consumer never surfaces them.
- **What changed.** Two complementary surfaces on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` (gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`):
  1. **In-stream textual marker** via `onAssistantDelta` ÔÇö `\n\n[HH:MM:SS] ­čŤá´ŞĆ ToolName: detail\n` followed by a rolling `_ Ns ÔÇö HH:MM:SS_` indicator that refreshes every 8s, stripped on the next `text_delta` or on turn end.
  2. **Structured `stream: "tool"` agent event** (`{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}`) via a new optional `onToolEvent` callback ÔÇö consumed by `cli-runner/execute.ts` and forwarded to the agent event bus for downstream tool-tile UIs.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect({backend, providerId})` ÔÇö no impact on non-Claude CLI backends. Existing `onAssistantDelta` callers are unaffected. `onToolEvent` is opt-in. No tool-output / tool-result event in this PR (the result is reflected in claude's next `text_delta` and the timer-strip handles UX). Tool args sanitized via `sanitizeToolArgs` before either path.
- **AI-assisted:** yes ÔÇö drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** tool calls during a Claude turn now produce both an in-stream textual marker (with rolling 8s timer indicator) and a structured `stream: "tool"` agent event, when `backend.jsonlDialect === "claude-stream-json"` or `providerId === "claude-cli"`.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142, Opus 4.7, live OpenClaw gateway driving the `claude-cli` backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`; live Telegram turn that invokes one or more tools (Read / Bash / Grep / WebFetch); observe the textual marker, the 8s tick refresh, and the marker stripping plus `\n\n` separator when assistant text resumes; verify the `stream: "tool"` agent event in `emitAgentEvent` logs.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - Telegram screenshot of a live turn (rolling-timer rendered to the end-user surface): ![telegram rolling-timer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets/pr-82285/.proof/telegram-rolling-timer.jpg). Every `[HH:MM:SS] ­čŤá´ŞĆ Bash: ÔÇŽ` line in the screenshot is one `emitTool` firing in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` against `claude-cli`.
  - Typecheck clean: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`.
  - The markers are an in-flight overlay delivered via `onAssistantDelta`, not persisted to `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl` (which stores claude's structured `result`) or `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` at default verbosity ÔÇö Telegram is the canonical capture surface.
- **Observed result after fix:** tool calls render as `[HH:MM:SS] ­čŤá´ŞĆ ToolName: detail` blocks in the assistant text, with the live timer ticking every 8s. When claude resumes streaming text, the timer is stripped and the text continues from a clean `\n\n` boundary. Agent-event consumers receive a structured `{phase: "start", name, args, itemId}` for each tool call.
- **What was not tested:** end-to-end against a dashboard / external listener for the `stream: "tool"` event surface (event is emitted, visible in `emitAgentEvent` logs, but no downstream UI verified beyond that); Linux/macOS execution (developed on Windows only); bundled MCP tool paths (`server_tool_use` / `mcp_tool_use` content-block types are matched in the tracker but I have not driven a live MCP-tool turn through it); focused unit test for `createClaudeToolUseTracker` is not added yet (see Regression Test Plan).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Adds opt-in `onToolEvent` callback on `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` + in-stream textual marker over the existing `onAssistantDelta`.
- Builds on #81851 (the `claude-cli-interactive` backend / `stream_event` surface this rendering consumes).

## Root Cause

N/A ÔÇö feature PR. The condition this addresses (no tool-call UX during turns) is a property of the Claude CLI JSONL surface (tool calls live in `stream_event`, never in `assistant_text` deltas), not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` ÔÇö add a `describe("tool-use tracker", ÔÇŽ)` block.
- **Scenario locked in:** simulated `stream_event` sequence ÔÇö `message_start` Ôćĺ `content_block_start (tool_use, name: "Read")` Ôćĺ 3├Ś `content_block_delta (input_json_delta partial)` Ôćĺ `content_block_stop` ÔÇö asserts the resulting `ClaudeToolEvent` carries the reassembled `args.file_path`, the right `itemId`, and that the parser's `assistantText` now includes the `[HH:MM:SS] ­čŤá´ŞĆ Read: <path>` line.
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode (regression of partial-JSON accumulation, wrong `content_block_stop` dispatch, verbose-gating drift) is fully exercisable by calling the parser's `push()` with a canned JSONL transcript ÔÇö no live claude needed. Runs in ms.
- **Status:** behaviour is exercised live (see proof above); the frozen-transcript unit test above is the planned guardrail to land with this change.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** partial-JSON accumulation in `createClaudeToolUseTracker` could drop or corrupt tool args if `content_block_stop` fires before all `input_json_delta` chunks arrive.
  - **Mitigation:** tracker holds the entry until `content_block_stop`, then parses `entry.input ?? entry.partialJson` via `parseClaudeToolArgs`. The pending unit test (`cli-output.test.ts`) locks in the contract for partial-JSON reassembly.
- **Risk:** rolling-timer `setInterval` leaks if a `content_block_stop` arrives but no subsequent `text_delta` fires (e.g. turn aborts mid-tool).
  - **Mitigation:** `clearToolKeepalive()` is also called on `result`, on `finish()`, and on the next `text_delta`. The parser closure owns the interval handle and explicitly clears it on every terminal path.
- **Risk:** tool args contain sensitive content (file paths, credentials in `WebFetch` URLs, etc.) and surface in both Telegram and agent events.
  - **Mitigation:** `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates and redacts known-sensitive keys before either emission path. Verbose gating (`shouldInjectToolInlineMarkers`) lets sessions opt out of inline markers without losing the structured event.
- **Risk:** the inline marker rides on `onAssistantDelta` ÔÇö any consumer that strict-parses assistant text as Markdown could choke on the `_ Ns ÔÇö HH:MM:SS_` indicator.
  - **Mitigation:** marker uses a deliberately conservative `\n\n[ÔÇŽ] ­čŤá´ŞĆ ÔÇŽ\n` shape that's Markdown-safe (Telegram renders it verbatim). Consumers wanting strict text can ignore by subscribing only to the structured `stream: "tool"` event.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** No.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No ÔÇö same allowed-tools set; `sanitizeToolArgs` truncates args before emission.
- **New/changed network calls:** No.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No ÔÇö same tools claude already executes; this PR only renders that they're in flight.
- **Data access scope changed:** No ÔÇö tracker reads `stream_event` records the parser already processes.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: gated by `usesClaudeStreamJsonDialect`; non-Claude backends unaffected.
- `onToolEvent` is an additive optional callback ÔÇö existing parser callers don't need to wire it up.
- No new config, no new env vars. Disable by not subscribing to the new event surface.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
